assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Andrey, I saw that Northstar has 45 contractors across complex geographies and a legal review coming up — and that payment sat stuck for 23 days. Mellow's scope: KYC, documents, payment chain, audit trail. We're clear on what we don't take on — misclassification risk stays on your side. Does this speak to what you're dealing with before the review?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

transcript.text: Andrey, I saw that Northstar has 45 contractors across complex geographies and a legal review coming up — and that payment sat stuck for 23 days. Mellow's scope: KYC, documents, payment chain, audit trail. We're clear on what we don't take on — misclassification risk stays on your side. Does this speak to what you're dealing with before the review?
Good. Then without preamble: what Mellow controls, what it doesn't, and how that's substantiated?

The current setup stops being defensible in two scenarios: a payment gets stuck with no SLA path — or diligence comes and there's no audit trail. Your current setup has both risks. Mellow removes them specifically.
Ok. Send me a brief written summary: payment flow, audit story, scope boundary.

On SLA: a named escalation owner, defined response time in the contract, incident trail documented. Not general support — a process with accountability. Which part of this maps most directly to your situation?
Too many declarations. What specifically changes in my process, and in what timeframe?
